signals.title: NZD/USD — TIER-1 Headline + CB Divergence + Positioning
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5688 facing acute volatility as ANZ and shadow board polls signal a 'line-ball' RBNZ hike to 2.50% on July 8. COT LF net short -23,314 (21.6% long) represents a significant spec fade of the hawkish OIS bias. With a -0.83% real rate (most accommodative in G10), a failure to hike risks a deep liquidation, while a 25bp move triggers a massive short-squeeze. NZD/USD 1M IV at 27.5% vs HV30 6.9% (VRP +20.6%) confirms the market is paying an extreme premium for protection ahead of the decision.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Options Skew + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 161.81 holding above the 160.00 psychological level where BoJ intervention risk remains critical. Despite a +275bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage sustaining the bid, the options market is aggressively hedging downside with a 25d RR of -1.67 (strong put skew). JPY COT LF net short -115,033 (28.4% long) shows crowded carry positioning vulnerable to a sharp unwind if the BoJ shifts stance at the July 31 meeting. VIX at 15.8 suggests carry remains the dominant regime until volatility spikes.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Retail Divergence + Correlation Break
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4210 showing extreme retail sentiment divergence with 93% of retail traders short against a net short COT position (LF net -87,096, 20.6% long). This retail-institutional alignment in direction (both shorting the USD leg) typically precedes a squeeze higher. The USD/CAD vs WTI correlation has broken to -0.876 (z = -2.06σ), indicating the pair is decoupling from oil prices to follow the +150bp USD carry advantage and negative CAD economic surprises (CESI -6).

signals.title: EUR/USD — Volatility Premium + Data Divergence
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1432 remains structurally capped by a +150bp Fed-ECB carry spread. While ECB's Nagel and Moulin signal a 'hawkish hold' vigilance, the EUR CESI at -11 (strong negative) vs USD CESI at +282 (strong positive) highlights a widening fundamental gap. Options market reflects caution with a +2.4% VRP (IV 7.1% vs HV30 4.7%), suggesting traders are paying for protection against a break of the 1M range despite neutral 25d RR (-0.26).
